_notes.labBounds: CIELAB (D65) axis-aligned box. Every resolved color is converted to L*a*b*, then each of L*, a*, b* is clamped to these ranges before converting to sRGB hex. L* is lightness (0 ≈ black, 100 ≈ white). a* runs green (−) to red (+). b* runs blue (−) to yellow (+). Real displayable colors usually sit well inside ±100 on a*/b*; this file uses a mid L* band (35–88) so swatches stay off pure black/white.
_notes.majors: Each key is a major category id. Value is an sRGB hex string (e.g. "#d1767f"). For major-only lookups (`getColorForMajor("food")` or `getColorForCategory("food")` with no colon), that hex is converted to LAB, clipped by labBounds, then back to hex. For full paths, the resolver builds a region around that seed (half-extents ∩ labBounds) and places non-explicit minors on an L* sweep plus a*/b* grid.
_notes.explicit: Keys may be a full path "major:minor" or a major-only id "major". Per-path overrides win; if only "major" is set, every minor under that major uses that color (no per-minor LAB grid). Values: hex string or { "hex" } / { "lab" }. Hex is normalized #rrggbb; lab-only entries skip labBounds on encode. Generated minors (when no major-level explicit) use LAB internally and are clipped by labBounds.
